checks.name: identical-file-hash-comparison
checks.detail: Compared 9 financial-skill files with 456 files under Posterly; no identical SHA-256 file hashes were found.

checks.name: posterly-specific-identifiers
checks.detail: No Posterly, Trackio, AGPL, ARIS, poster_check, render_preview, run_gates, data-measure-role, or Posterly template identifiers occur in the financial skill or generated package.

checks.name: dependency-boundary
checks.detail: The financial scripts import Python standard-library modules plus the independently installed Playwright package; they do not import Posterly modules or tools.

checks.name: template-boundary
checks.detail: The financial template is a multi-page semantic financial document with original selectors and component structure; it does not use Posterly templates or measurement-role contracts.

checks.name: standalone-render
checks.detail: The final PDF was rerendered with a standalone temporary Playwright environment rather than the Posterly project environment.

checks.name: pdf-content-and-metadata
checks.detail: Extracted PDF text and PDF metadata contain no Posterly attribution, AGPL notice, Posterly identifier, or Posterly asset reference.

notes: This is a technical provenance review, not a legal opinion.
Generic ideas such as structured input validation, paged HTML, PDF rendering, and quality controls are not evidence of copied source code or templates.
No license has been assigned to the new financial skill; choose its distribution license separately before publishing it.
